metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from her hand. It fell to the floor. It broke into many pieces. She swept them up carefully.
The alarm clock rang at six in the morning. He got out of bed slowly. He walked to the kitchen in his slippers. He made a pot of strong coffee.
Dark clouds gathered over the small town. Rain started to fall very heavily. The streets flooded within an hour. People rushed indoors to stay dry.
She planted tomato seeds in the garden soil. She watered them every single day. Green sprouts appeared after one week. By summer she had ripe red tomatoes.
The dog found a bone buried in the yard. It carried the bone to its bed. It chewed on the bone for several hours. It fell asleep with the bone nearby.
He studied hard for the final math exam. He reviewed every chapter in the textbook twice. He walked into the test feeling very confident. He earned the highest score in the class.
The baker mixed flour, eggs, and sugar together. She poured the batter into a round pan. She placed the pan in the hot oven. A delicious cake was ready an hour later.
The boy climbed the tall oak tree in the yard. A branch cracked loudly under his weight. He lost his grip and fell to the ground. His father rushed over to help him up.
She opened the letter from the university admissions office. She read the first line of the letter carefully. A huge smile spread slowly across her face. She had been accepted into the program.
The car ran out of gas on the highway. The driver pulled over to the right shoulder. He called a friend on his phone for help. His friend arrived twenty minutes later with gas.
The cat spotted a small mouse near the wall. It crouched low to the ground and moved silently. It leaped forward with great speed and precision. The mouse escaped through a tiny hole in the wall.

Snow fell heavily throughout the long cold night. The roads were completely covered by early morning. Schools announced closures for the entire day. Children went outside to build snowmen in the yard.
She forgot her umbrella at home that morning. A sudden rainstorm caught her walking outside. She ran quickly to the nearest shop for shelter. She bought a cheap umbrella before heading back out.
The fisherman cast his line far into the lake. He waited patiently for a very long time. The line suddenly jerked and pulled very tight. He reeled in a large silver fish.
The phone buzzed with a new text message. She picked it up and read the message quickly. She laughed out loud at the funny joke. She forwarded the message to her best friend.
He tripped on a loose stone in the path. He fell hard and scraped his knee badly. He limped to the nearest bench and sat down. He cleaned the wound carefully with a tissue.

The teacher wrote a math problem on the board. The students copied it into their notebooks quietly. They worked on the problem for about ten minutes. One student raised her hand with the correct answer.

She mixed several paint colors on her palette carefully. She applied broad strokes across the blank white canvas. She added fine details with a very thin brush. A beautiful landscape took shape before her eyes.

The train arrived at the station right on time. Passengers stepped off the train onto the platform. New passengers quickly boarded and found their seats. The train departed again just three minutes later.
He plugged in the old lamp he found in storage. The bulb flickered a few times very weakly. Then it burst with a small sharp pop. He unplugged it and threw the broken bulb away.
The hikers set out early on a cool morning. They followed a narrow trail through the dense woods. They reached the mountain summit by noon that day. They sat down and enjoyed the wide open view.
She dropped her phone into the swimming pool accidentally. She reached in and pulled it out very quickly. The screen was black and would not turn on. She took it to a repair shop downtown.
The wind knocked over the wooden fence in the yard. Boards lay scattered across the wet green grass. He gathered the broken pieces after the storm passed. He rebuilt the fence the following weekend.
The toddler stacked colorful blocks into a tall tower. The tower wobbled slightly near the very top. She placed one more block on the unsteady pile. The whole tower came crashing down with a clatter.
He noticed a flat tire on his old bicycle. He flipped the bike upside down on the sidewalk. He removed the damaged tire carefully with a wrench. He replaced it with a brand new tire.

The power went out during the family evening meal. They searched the drawers for candles in the dark. They lit several candles around the kitchen table. They finished their dinner together by warm candlelight.
She heard a strange noise coming from the attic. She grabbed a flashlight from the kitchen drawer. She climbed the stairs and opened the attic door. A small bird was trapped near the dusty window.
The chef chopped fresh vegetables for the evening soup. He added them to a large pot of boiling water. He stirred the pot and added salt and pepper. The soup was served hot to the waiting guests.

The kite caught a strong gust of spring wind. It soared high above the wide open grassy field. The string snapped suddenly without any warning at all. The kite drifted away slowly over the distant trees.
He saved money from his weekend job every month. After a full year he had enough for a trip. He booked a flight to a warm tropical island. He spent a wonderful relaxing week on the beach.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.
Mars has two small moons orbiting around it. Jupiter has a giant red storm on its surface. Saturn is surrounded by bright rings of ice. Neptune appears deep blue when seen from a distance.

Spiders have eight legs and can spin silk webs. Ants live in large organized colonies underground. Butterflies go through a stage called metamorphosis. Beetles have hard protective shells covering their wings.
Tokyo is the capital city of Japan. Canberra is the capital city of Australia. Ottawa is the capital city of Canada. Brasilia is the capital city of Brazil.
Copper is a very good conductor of electricity. Gold does not rust or corrode over time. Iron is attracted to magnets quite strongly. Aluminum is lightweight and resists corrosion very well.

Penguins cannot fly but they swim very well. Ostriches are the largest living birds on Earth today. Hummingbirds can hover in one place while feeding on nectar. Owls can rotate their heads almost fully around.

Roses are often given as gifts on special occasions. Sunflowers turn their heads to follow the sunlight. Cacti store water inside their thick green stems. Bamboo can grow several feet in just one day.

French is an official language in many African countries. Mandarin Chinese uses tonal differences to change word meaning. Arabic script is written from right to left. Portuguese is widely spoken all across South America.

The heart pumps blood throughout the entire human body. The lungs take in oxygen from the air we breathe. The kidneys filter waste products out of the blood. The liver helps break down food and removes harmful toxins.

Basketball was created in the United States in 1891. Chess originated in India many centuries ago. Judo was developed in Japan in the late 1800s. Table tennis became popular in England during the 1880s.

Honey never spoils if it is stored in sealed containers. Olive oil has been used in cooking for thousands of years. Cinnamon comes from the bark of certain tropical trees. Vanilla is extracted from the pods of a special orchid.

An Olympic swimming pool is exactly 50 meters in length. A marathon race covers a distance of about 42 kilometers. A basketball court measures roughly 28 meters from end to end. A standard soccer field is approximately 100 meters long.

Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius under normal conditions. Sound travels faster through water than it does through air. Light travels at roughly 300,000 kilometers every single second. Gravity on the moon is much weaker than on Earth.

Elephants communicate using very low rumbling sounds. Dolphins use clicking sounds to locate objects under water. Wolves howl to signal their location to distant pack members. Frogs croak loudly during the warm spring mating season.

Carrots are rich in vitamin A for healthy eyesight. Oranges contain high amounts of vitamin C for immunity. Bananas provide potassium needed for proper muscle function. Spinach is a very good source of iron and calcium.

The Great Wall of China stretches for thousands of miles. The Colosseum in Rome once held around 50,000 spectators. The Pyramids of Giza were built over 4,000 years ago. Machu Picchu sits high up in the Andes mountains.

Maple trees produce sweet sap used to make syrup. Oak trees can live for several hundred years easily. Pine trees keep their green needles all throughout the winter. Willow trees tend to grow near rivers and ponds.

Cotton is a natural fiber that grows in warm climates. Silk is produced by silkworms spinning their tiny cocoons. Wool comes from the fleece of sheep after they are sheared. Linen is made from the strong fibers of flax plants.

A compass needle always points toward magnetic north. A thermometer measures temperature using expanding liquid inside glass. A barometer detects changes in the atmospheric air pressure. A sundial tells time by the shifting position of shadows.

Sweden is known for its long and dark cold winters. Egypt has a hot and mostly dry climate all year. Brazil contains large areas of dense tropical rainforest. Iceland has many active volcanoes and natural hot springs.

Sharks have lived on Earth for hundreds of millions of years. Octopuses have three hearts that pump blue colored blood. Seahorses are among the very slowest swimmers in the ocean. Jellyfish have no brain, no heart, and no bones.

Brick houses provide good insulation from outside temperatures. Log cabins are constructed by stacking wooden logs horizontally. Igloos are built from blocks of hard compacted snow. Thatched roofs are made using dried straw or reed bundles.

A piano has 88 black and white keys in a row. A standard guitar has six strings tuned to different notes. A trumpet produces sound through vibrating lip pressure in a mouthpiece. An accordion makes music by squeezing air through small metal reeds.

Mercury is the closest planet to our sun. Venus rotates in the opposite direction from most other planets. Uranus tilts almost completely on its side as it orbits. Mars has the tallest known volcano in our solar system.

The human body contains a total of 206 bones. Fingernails grow noticeably faster than toenails on average. Adults have 32 teeth including their four wisdom teeth. The tongue is one of the strongest muscles relative to size.

Cheetahs can run faster than any other land animal alive. Sloths move so slowly that green algae grows on their fur. Blue whales are the largest animals ever to have lived. Giraffes have the same number of neck bones as humans.

The Dead Sea is so salty that swimmers float very easily. Lake Victoria is the largest lake on the African continent. The Danube River flows through ten different European countries. The Yangtze is the longest river on the Asian continent.

Bees are essential pollinators for many important food crops. Ladybugs eat aphids that can damage young garden plants. Fireflies produce light through a chemical reaction inside their bodies. Dragonflies are able to fly in any direction including backward.

Paper was first invented in ancient China many centuries ago. Concrete was used by the Romans to build lasting structures. Rubber comes from the sticky sap of tropical rubber trees. Glass is made by heating sand to extremely high temperatures.

Kenya is located on the eastern coast of Africa. Peru borders the Pacific Ocean on the west of South America. Thailand lies in the heart of mainland Southeast Asia. Norway stretches along the northwestern coast of northern Europe.
